chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وَفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Nazool (Arrival) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> demise

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (194/ 5)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (194/ 5)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام )

text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ إِذْ لَا كَانَ فَخَلَقَ الْكَانَ وَ الْمَكَانَ وَ خَلَقَ نُورَ الْأَنْوَارِ الَّذِي نُوِّرَتْ مِنْهُ الْأَنْوَارُ وَ أَجْرَى فِيهِ مِنْ نُورِهِ الَّذِي نُوِّرَتْ مِنْهُ الْأَنْوَارُ وَ هُوَ النُّورُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنْهُ مُحَمَّداً وَ عَلِيّاً فَلَمْ يَزَالَا نُورَيْنِ أَوَّلَيْنِ إِذْ لَا شَيْ‏ءَ كُوِّنَ قَبْلَهُمَا فَلَمْ يَزَالَا يَجْرِيَانِ طَاهِرَيْنِ مُطَهَّرَيْنِ فِي الْأَصْلَابِ الطَّاهِرَةِ حَتَّى افْتَرَقَا فِي أَطْهَرِ طَاهِرَيْنِ فِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام ) .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Al Husayn Bin Abdullah Al Sagheer, from Muhammad Bin Ibrahim Al Ja’fary, from Ahmad Bin Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah Bin Umar,

(It has been narrated) son of Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup>, from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Existed when there was no other existence. So He<sup>azwj</sup> Created the existence (beings), and the space, and Created the Light of the light from which the lights are illuminated, and Made to flow into it from His<sup>azwj</sup> Own Light from which the lights lit up, and it is the Light from which were created Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> and Ali<sup>asws</sup>. So they<sup>asws</sup> did not cease to be two former lights when there was nothing existing before the two of them<sup>asws</sup>. Thus, they both<sup>asws</sup> did not cease to flow as clean, having been Cleaned, into the clean ‘الْأَصْلَابِ’, until they both<sup>asws</sup> separated in the most clean of the two clean (linages), in Abdullah<sup>as</sup> and Abu Talib<sup>as</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 8. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Walid who has said that he heard Yunus ibn ya‘qub narrated from Sinan ibn Turayf who has said the following. “I heard abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) say, ‘We are the first family whose names Allah has exalted. When He created the heavens and earth He commanded an announcer to proclaim. “I testify that there is no lord besides Allah three times. I testify that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, three times and that Ali is the Amir al-Mu’minin Ali (a.s.) (Leader with Divine Authority) in all truth, three times.”
